channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Reliance Retail Buys Priyanka Chopra Jonas's Anomaly Haircare Brand
item.description: Reliance Retail announced Thursday it has acquired Anomaly, the haircare brand co-founded by Priyanka Chopra Jonas. The deal gives Reliance full ownership of the brand's intellectual property and trademarks, boosting its beauty offerings. Anomaly, known for clean, vegan products, will be scaled across Reliance's extensive retail and digital platforms, with India as the main market.

item.title: Oolka Secures $14M to Build AI Financial Guides for India
item.description: Fintech startup Oolka has raised $14 million in a Series A round led by Accel. The company uses AI-powered agents to help Indian consumers with their finances, aiming to improve financial literacy beyond simple transactions. Oolka, which already serves over 6 million users and has $2.5 million in annual recurring revenue, plans to use the new funds to enhance its AI tools and build a comprehensive financial system for India's digital economy.

item.title: Ctruh Lands $2.5M Seed to Build Spatial Internet Foundation
item.description: Deep-tech firm Ctruh has secured $2.5 million in seed funding, co-led by Inflection Point Ventures and Avinya Ventures. The capital will accelerate R&D for its browser-native 3D engine and VersaAI platform, and fuel go-to-market expansion into the U.S. and UAE. This funding targets the burgeoning spatial internet market, aiming to simplify the creation and deployment of immersive experiences for businesses of all sizes, bridging the gap between advanced XR technology and practical commercial application.

item.title: Union Mutual Fund Launches Arthaya Equity Long Short Fund Under SEBI SIF Framework
item.description: Union Mutual Fund has launched the Arthaya Equity Long Short Fund, marking the start of SEBI's Specialized Investment Fund (SIF) framework. This allows broader investor access to advanced, hedged equity strategies. The fund aims to generate returns through market cycles by holding both long and short equity positions, a strategy popular in volatile markets. This launch reflects a trend of fund houses using the new SIF structure for unique products.

item.title: Bajaj Finserv Takes Full Insurance Control After Record FY26 Results
item.description: Bajaj Finserv reported a landmark fiscal year 2026, achieving record consolidated total income of ₹150,530 crore and net profit of ₹9,801 crore. A key strategic move was the complete acquisition of Allianz's stake in its life and general insurance businesses, granting full operational control. The company recommended a dividend of ₹1.50 per share, including a special centenary payout. Its lending arm, Bajaj Finance, saw its loan book grow to over ₹5 lakh crore, while new investments in health and digital platforms continue. This shift to full ownership marks a significant pivot, giving the group greater strategic flexibility and full responsibility for execution.

item.title: Adani Enterprises Posts Loss, Plans ₹15,000 Crore Share Sale
item.description: Adani Enterprises reported a ₹220.7 crore net loss for Q4 FY26, a sharp drop from last year's profit, despite a 20.3% revenue increase to ₹32,439.3 crore. The company plans to raise up to ₹15,000 crore through equity issuance, pending shareholder approval, and recommended a ₹1.30 per share dividend. For the full year FY26, income grew 3% to ₹1,02,943 crore, with stable EBITDA.

item.title: Dr. Lal PathLabs: Revenue Soars, Profits Dip Amid Margin Squeeze
item.description: Dr. Lal PathLabs announced mixed fourth-quarter results, showcasing a robust 16.6% revenue increase to ₹702.7 crore, but net profit declined 15.2% to ₹131.3 crore. Profitability was pressured as margins slipped to 26.6% from 28% year-over-year due to escalating operational costs. Despite these headwinds, the company proposed a ₹4 per share final dividend. The stock closed down 1.7% on April 30, 2026. This divergence between topline growth and bottomline performance signals underlying challenges in cost management and profitability sustainability within the diagnostic sector.

item.title: Brookfield's Altius India Tower IPO Eyes $630 Million
item.description: Brookfield Asset Management plans to list its Indian telecom tower unit, Altius Telecom Infrastructure Trust, in an IPO that could raise up to $630 million. Altius is India's largest tower operator with over 257,000 sites. The offering follows a strong year for Indian Infrastructure Investment Trusts (InvITs), which have significantly beaten market benchmarks, highlighting investor interest in digital infrastructure.

item.title: Bajaj Finserv Q4 Profit Up 5%, Net Income Surges 21%, Dividend Recommended
item.description: Bajaj Finserv announced a 5% year-on-year rise in consolidated net profit for the March quarter of FY26, reaching ₹2,539 crore. Net income jumped 21% to ₹14,209 crore, fueled by a 17% increase in interest income. The board recommended a dividend of ₹1.50 per equity share, including a special payout for the Bajaj Group's 100th anniversary. The company also finalized acquiring Allianz SE's stake in its insurance units.

item.title: Maharashtra AI Policy Targets 150,000 Jobs, ₹10K Crore Investment by 2031
item.description: Maharashtra has approved its AI Policy 2026, targeting ₹10,000 crore in investments and creating 150,000 jobs by 2031. The policy includes establishing AI innovation hubs, Centres of Excellence, and a dedicated AI Mission. It also aims to train 200,000 individuals, support 5,000 MSMEs, and launch a ₹500 crore AI startup fund to boost innovation.

item.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank: Q4 Results Due Amidst Deepening Margin Pressure
item.description: Kotak Mahindra Bank's board meets Saturday, May 2nd, to review Q4FY26 results. Investors are focused on Net Interest Margins (NIMs) which have seen steady declines, alongside net interest income and profitability. Despite a recent stock recovery, the bank's year-to-date performance is down, signaling caution. The meeting will also cover the FY27 outlook and dividend plans.

item.title: PFRDA Slashes NPS Fees for Subscribers From July 1, 2026
item.description: The Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA) has issued a new circular on April 29, 2026, detailing significant revisions to Central Recordkeeping Agency (CRA) charges for National Pension System (NPS) subscribers. Effective July 1, 2026, Annual Maintenance Charges (AMC) for Tier II NPS accounts will align with Tier I rates. Dormant accounts will face a reduced 10% AMC, while accounts with a nil balance under APY and NPS-Lite will have zero AMC. PRAN opening charges are now a one-time fee. These changes aim to simplify costs for subscribers and require operational adjustments from CRAs like Protean and KFintech.

item.title: India's Health Insurers Shift to Wellness Amid Major Roadblocks
item.description: India's health insurance industry is moving towards wellness-focused plans, integrating technology like AI and wearables. But this shift faces major hurdles. Key challenges include standardizing health data, clarifying privacy rules, proving that preventive care truly improves health, and managing potentially higher costs. The industry's ambitious goal may be outrunning its practical capacity, leading to questions about affordability and access for all.

item.title: India-NZ FTA: Operational Readiness Beats Tariff Cuts for Exporters
item.description: India's Free Trade Agreement with New Zealand offers major export opportunities. However, trade finance expert Siddharth Shankar stresses that operational readiness, not just tariff cuts, will shape the benefits. He explains that Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) must meet global standards in technology, quality, and supply chain discipline to succeed. The agreement includes a $20 billion New Zealand investment pledge, which requires startups to manage capital carefully to avoid excessive equity loss. Increased cross-border mobility for professionals also offers chances to boost national skills. While some sectors like dairy and agriculture have protections, all companies must work within the new regulations.

item.title: Indian Investors Target Nasdaq 100 Funds: Understand the Risks
item.description: Indian investors are looking to Nasdaq 100 funds for global tech growth. Funds from Motilal Oswal, ICICI Pru, Navi, Invesco, and Axis provide rupee access to tech giants. However, investors should understand fund structures, SEBI's investment limits, concentration risks, Nasdaq's volatility, and currency changes for smart diversification.

item.title: Dubai Lifts Property Visa Investment Minimum to Boost Real Estate
item.description: Dubai has eliminated the minimum property investment requirement for its two-year property-linked resident visa. The Emirate scrapped the AED 750,000 threshold for individual buyers, now allowing entry with any property value. For jointly owned assets, the minimum stake per investor has been set at AED 400,000. This strategic move aims to revitalize the real estate market and attract a broader range of international investors.

item.title: Kajaria Ceramics Falls Despite Record Earnings, Rs 297 Cr Buyback
item.description: Kajaria Ceramics announced a significant Rs 297 crore share buyback and a Rs 6 per share final dividend alongside strong fourth-quarter earnings. Net profit quadrupled year-on-year to Rs 156.6 crore, with revenue up 12.4% and EBITDA margins expanding sharply. Despite these positives, the company's stock fell nearly 5% intraday, underperforming the broader market, suggesting investors are weighing rich valuations against industry challenges.

item.title: Supreme Court Tackles NCLT Delays, IBC Credibility Tested
item.description: The Supreme Court is reviewing significant delays in approvals at the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT), citing critical infrastructure and staff shortages. This scrutiny questions the effectiveness of India's Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC), potentially impacting investor confidence and distressed asset resolution.

item.title: Cholamandalam Finance Posts 29.5% Profit Jump Amid NBFC Sector Pressures
item.description: Cholamandalam Investment (CIFC) saw its Q4 FY26 net profit surge 29.5% to ₹1,640.7 crore and Net Interest Income climb 26% to ₹3,855 crore. Assets Under Management (AUM) grew 21% to ₹2.43 lakh crore. Despite these strong results, the stock saw only modest gains, as investors weigh sector cost pressures and risks from the company's expansion efforts.

item.title: India's Food Brands Use Storytelling to Drive Growth
item.description: India's food and drink market is entering a new growth phase. The latest Godrej Food Trends Report highlights storytelling—focusing on origin and culture—as the main way to stand out. Brands need emotional connections to capture value.

item.title: L&T Finance Bets on AI for ₹3 Lakh Crore Target, Faces Credit Cost and Valuation Tests
item.description: L&T Finance launched 'Lakshya 2031' to grow its loan book to ₹3 lakh crore by 2031, targeting high growth and profitability. The plan heavily uses AI for underwriting and efficiency, aiming for cost savings. However, the company faces challenges in reaching below 2% credit costs amid increased unsecured lending, a P/E ratio above its historical average, and potential market pressures.

item.title: NALCO Profit Up 7%, Declares ₹2 Dividend with May 8 Record Date
item.description: National Aluminium Company (NALCO) reported a 7.3% rise in fourth-quarter profit for FY26, reaching ₹1,718 crore on ₹5,013 crore revenue. The company also declared a ₹2 per share interim dividend, with May 8, 2026, as the record date.

item.title: India NBFCs Face Rising Costs, Pivot to Banks Amid Global Uncertainty
item.description: India Ratings expects funding costs for NBFCs and HFCs to rise slightly in the medium term. This is due to global tensions, economic instability, and banks' slow pass-through of policy rate cuts. The sector is also changing its funding sources, relying more on bank loans instead of capital markets, driven by more stable pricing from banks and caution in international markets. External borrowing will likely stay low, while bank lending to NBFCs is accelerating.

item.title: Energy Transition Roadmap Faces Staggering Finance Gap
item.description: Fifty-seven nations have pledged a concrete roadmap to accelerate the energy transition, moving beyond targets to implementation. Global investment in transition technologies hit a record $2.4 trillion in 2024, yet this capital flow is heavily concentrated in advanced economies and China. Developing nations, crucial to the transition, received only 15% of global clean energy spending, facing significant debt constraints and a lack of affordable finance. The pace of renewable energy growth, while substantial, is reportedly slowing, raising doubts about meeting ambitious 2030 climate goals amid persistent systemic and financial obstacles.

item.title: Meesho Stock Surges 12% on JP Morgan 'Overweight' Call
item.description: Meesho shares jumped about 12% after JP Morgan initiated coverage with an 'Overweight' rating and a ₹215 price target. The brokerage highlighted Meesho's strong Net Merchandise Value (NMV) growth potential, opportunities to increase advertising revenue, and its improving logistics network through Valmo. However, questions remain about its past losses, competition from Flipkart and Amazon India, and whether its aggressive growth targets are sustainable in a fragmented retail market.

item.title: Goldman Sachs: $5,400 Gold Target Faces Geopolitical Jitters
item.description: Goldman Sachs predicts gold will reach $5,400 an ounce by the end of 2026, thanks to steady central bank buying and anticipated Federal Reserve rate cuts. But, global tensions and worries about market liquidations are creating significant short-term volatility. While official demand provides a solid foundation for the future, gold prices are navigating immediate geopolitical risks alongside their long-term potential.

item.title: India Durables Sales Surge on Heatwave, Cost Pressures Mount
item.description: India's consumer durables and electricals sector is seeing a strong rebound, driven by intense heatwaves that have boosted demand, especially in the south. However, April sales volumes are expected to be flat year-on-year. Premium product segments are doing well, but companies are grappling with ongoing commodity inflation and currency pressures. This means price hikes of 6-8%, with more possible, are needed to safeguard margins. While inventories are manageable, companies are stocking cautiously due to unpredictable weather.

item.title: OnEMI IPO Opens: High Growth Bets on 94% Unsecured Loans
item.description: OnEMI Technology's ₹925.92 crore IPO opens today to fund its subsidiary Si Creva. The digital lender boasts strong user growth, but faces investor caution over its 94% unsecured loan portfolio, regulatory changes, and a recent dip in FY25 revenue.

item.title: Adani Ports Revenue Jumps 26% But Margins Shrink, Sparking Investor Concern
item.description: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone (APSEZ) reported a 10.4% increase in March quarter net profit to ₹3,329 crore, fueled by a 26.5% revenue surge to ₹10,737 crore. However, EBITDA margins narrowed year-on-year to 56.2% from 58.9%, largely due to faster growth in lower-margin logistics and international businesses, alongside capital expenditure exceeding guidance. Despite strong annual results and cargo milestones, the stock dropped over 3.5% after the earnings release. Analysts remain optimistic with a 'Strong Buy' consensus and price targets suggesting upside.

item.title: Adani Ports Profit Jumps 10.5% on Cargo Boom Amid Shipping Disruptions
item.description: Adani Ports and SEZ (APSEZ) reported a 10.5% rise in net profit to ₹3,329 crore ($350 million) for the quarter ending March 31, 2026. The growth stemmed from a 13% surge in cargo volumes to 133.4 million metric tonnes, driving port revenue up 30.5%. Global shipping route disruptions helped divert cargo to APSEZ facilities, according to analysts. The company’s logistics segment grew a modest 2%, while investor focus remains on its valuation.

item.title: Exfinity Exits CloudSEK With 13x Return Amid AI Cybersecurity Boom
item.description: Exfinity Venture Partners has achieved a significant partial exit from its cybersecurity investment in CloudSEK, delivering a 13x return and over 40% IRR. CloudSEK, an AI-driven threat intelligence platform, has surpassed $15 million in ARR and is seeing robust international growth, particularly in the U.S. This move highlights the strength of Indian deep-tech and Exfinity's successful strategy of capitalizing on gains while retaining future upside in a rapidly evolving cybersecurity market.

item.title: IndiGo Flights Run On Time Amid Soaring Costs and Rivalry
item.description: IndiGo's on-time performance has sharply improved, reaching 88.7% in March after severe disruptions in December 2025. The airline continues to hold over 63% of the market share. However, it operates in a tough Indian aviation market facing high fuel costs, global instability, and strong competition from rivals like Air India and Akasa Air.

item.title: Indo-National Buys Defense Stake for ₹78 Cr Amid Financial Woes
item.description: Indo-National Limited is venturing into the defense electronics sector through a ₹78 crore acquisition of a 51% stake in Aidin Technologies Private Limited. This strategic pivot aims to establish a new growth engine beyond INL's core consumer electronics business. However, the move faces scrutiny as Indo-National grapples with its own declining revenues and persistent losses, while Aidin, though growing, operates in a specialized, capital-intensive niche.
